name: Murkha Dal
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the butter in a large saucepan over moderate heat.
2. Add the mustard seeds and cover the pan. When you can hear the seeds popping, add the onion, garlic, and ginger root.
3. Cook, uncovered, until the onion, garlic, and ginger are soft and the garlic is brown, about 7–8 minutes.
4. Stir in the turmeric and green chilies and cook for 1–2 minutes until the chilies soften slightly.
5. Add the lentils and cook for 2 minutes, stirring frequently, until the lentils begin to turn translucent.
6. Add the water, coconut milk, and salt. Stir well.
7. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 40 minutes, or until the desired consistency is reached. For a thinner consistency, 40 minutes will be appropriate; for a thicker consistency, cook longer.
8. If you plan to reheat the dal before eating it, cook for only 30 minutes to allow for reheating time.
9. Serve immediately while hot.
